lessons.name: Welcome to Artemis Support Program
lessons.description: Get oriented with the mission control interface and verify the rover connection.
lessons.template:
from lineRobot import Robot

print("ARTEMIS LTV SYSTEM DIAGNOSTIC")
print("Initializing hardware...")

# Initialize LTV unit
ltv_unit = Robot()

print("[TEST 1] Checking Main Drive...")
ltv_unit.move_forward_distance(15)
ltv_unit.move_backward_distance(15)

print("[TEST 2] Checking Steering Mechanism...")
ltv_unit.turn_left()
ltv_unit.turn_right()

print("DIAGNOSTIC COMPLETE. SYSTEMS NOMINAL.")
print("Welcome to the team!")

lessons.name: Test drive
lessons.description: Create a robot object and run your first movement command.

lessons.name: License to drive
lessons.description: Write a simple program independently using import, object creation, and movement commands.

lessons.name: Directional Movement
lessons.description: Use distance-based movement functions to move forward and backward precisely.

lessons.name: Sequential navigation
lessons.description: Plan a multi-step route and document your code using comments.

name: Sensors & Reactive Navigation
description: From Octoliner basics to conditional logic, arrays, LED feedback, and first autonomous line-following behaviors.

lessons.name: Introduction to Octoliner Sensor
lessons.description: Connect the Octoliner over I2C and read the central IR sensor to detect line contrast.

lessons.name: Visual Telemetry (LEDs)
lessons.description: Control onboard LEDs with GPIO to show real-time scan status.

lessons.name: Simple Line Follower
lessons.description: Build a relay (bang-bang) controller using scout sensors and motor speed commands.

name: Line Sensor Introduction
description: Understanding how IR line sensors work and detecting black lines using the Octoliner sensor array.

lessons.name: Line Sensor and Black Line Detection
lessons.description: This lesson focuses on understanding how an Octoliner sensor detects black lines using IR sensors and implementing black line detection.

name: Programming Fundamentals
description: Essential programming concepts including variables, conditional statements, loops, and arrays for robot control.

lessons.name: Introduction to Variables and Conditional Statements
lessons.description: Learn how to declare and use variables in programming, and apply conditional logic using if, else if, and else statements.

lessons.name: Loops and Conditional Logic
lessons.description: Learn how to repeat actions using for and while loops combined with conditional statements.

lessons.name: Arrays and Processing Data with Loops
lessons.description: Understand how to use arrays to store and analyze sensor data with systematic data collection.

lessons.name: MQTT Greeting of Variables
lessons.description: Declare variables of different types and broadcast them in a single MQTT status line.

lessons.name: Telemetry: Calculating Time
lessons.description: Compute mission travel time from distance and speed and format it as telemetry.

lessons.name: Sensor Log: List and Aggregates
lessons.description: Summarise a list of sensor readings by reporting the count, values, and average.

lessons.name: Working with Lists: Adding, Removing, and Measuring
lessons.description: Manipulate a mixed-type list and report its contents and length via MQTT.

name: Advanced Line Following
description: Advanced line following techniques including relay, P, PI, and PID controllers for smooth and accurate robot movement.

lessons.name: Relay, P, and PI Controllers
lessons.description: Understand how different types of controllers work — Relay, Proportional (P), and Proportional-Integral (PI) for line following.

lessons.name: P and PI Controllers
lessons.description: Learn about Proportional and Proportional-Integral controllers for improved line following performance.

lessons.name: PID Controller
lessons.description: Implement a complete PID controller combining proportional, integral, and derivative terms for optimal line following.

name: Lists, Dictionaries and More
description: Hands-on assignments that connect sensors, LEDs, PWM, and telemetry using MicroPython data structures.

lessons.name: Telemetry Heartbeat with Status Levels
lessons.description: Derive a health flag from readiness, speed, and battery data and include it in the STATUS heartbeat.

lessons.name: Line Sensor Reactive LEDs
lessons.description: Mirror Octoliner readings to the LEDs using lists and dictionaries, then report the snapshot over MQTT.

name: Exploration and Mapping
description: Exploration missions that reveal a foggy map, scan for minerals, and navigate by coordinates with visual overlays.

lessons.name: Fog of War Survey
lessons.description: Use a snake-pattern sweep to uncover a provided foggy map and stop once coverage passes 90%.

lessons.name: Docking
lessons.description: Implement an auto-docking procedure to navigate the robot to a charging station

name: Sandbox and robot info
description: Getting familiar with the platform and learning how to control the robot.

lessons.name: Sandbox
lessons.description: Introduction to the platform, listing all peripherals, describing what we will be working with, and outlining any restrictions on libraries and tag words.
lessons.template:
from lineRobot import Robot


robot = Robot()
print("Turning left")
robot.turn_left_angle(45)
print("Moving forward")
robot.move_forward_distance(20)
print("Turning right")
robot.turn_right()
print("Moving forward")
robot.move_forward_distance(20)
print("Turning right")
robot.turn_right()
print("Moving forward")
robot.move_forward_distance(20)
print("Turning right")
robot.turn_right()
print("Moving forward")
robot.move_forward_distance(20)
